hero.lede: Verbumia is a translation manager for product teams who treat copy like code. The SDK ships with a runtime missing-key handler that reports every untranslated string the moment a user sees a fallback — no more silent gaps in production.
hero.note: MIT-licensed SDK · MCP-native · No per-word billing
live.title: Live missing-key telemetry
live.subtitle: Every fallback the SDK serves is captured here in realtime.
live.empty: No misses yet. Click a trigger below to see one stream in.
live.trigger.title: Trigger a missing key
live.trigger.subtitle: These keys are intentionally absent from every locale.
install.title: Two snippets and you're translating.
features.title: Three things you won't find anywhere else
features.one.title: Runtime missing-key handler
features.one.body: The SDK reports every miss back to your dashboard with the exact key, namespace and locale — without a CI step or a static scan.
features.two.title: MCP-native
features.two.body: Your AI agents can read, propose and merge translations through a first-class MCP server — not a bolt-on REST wrapper.
features.three.title: Predictable pricing
features.three.body: Flat tiers from the Hobby plan onward. Unlimited languages, no per-word fees, no surprise bills the day before launch.
pricing.title: Pricing made for shipping
pricing.subtitle: Start free. Unlock unlimited languages on every paid tier.
pricing.free.name: Free
pricing.hobby.name: Hobby
pricing.pro.name: Pro
pricing.team.name: Team
footer.tagline: Verbumia · words that stay in sync.
footer.demo: This demo is open-source under MIT.

demo.title: Autoplay timeline
demo.subtitle: Cycles FR · EN · ES, fires the four canonical missing keys, then loops.
